payload.query: Presidents strive to influence Congress. Presidential popularity is often mentioned as a source of success. Review research on this issue. How has it progressed theoretically and methodologically? What is the current status of this hypothesized relationship? How important is popularity compared to other sources of presidential congressional influence?

articles.title: A Diminished Congress Weighs Whether to Reassert Its Power
articles.abstract: Lawmakers head into President Trump’s second year facing questions about whether they can reclaim congressional clout in the face of his power grab.
articles.snippet: Lawmakers head into President Trump’s second year facing questions about whether they can reclaim congressional clout in the face of his power grab.

articles.title: Jon Kyl, Former Arizona Senator, Says He Has Dementia
articles.abstract: The Republican served for almost three decades in Congress. He said he was withdrawing from public life after the diagnosis.
articles.snippet: The Republican served for almost three decades in Congress. He said he was withdrawing from public life after the diagnosis.

articles.title: Senate Advances Measure to Curb Trump’s Use of Force in Venezuela
articles.abstract: A handful of Republicans joined Democrats to allow the measure to move forward, reflecting concerns in both parties about the administration’s strategy.
articles.snippet: A handful of Republicans joined Democrats to allow the measure to move forward, reflecting concerns in both parties about the administration’s strategy.
articles.byline: By Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
keywords: United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Indiana Lawmakers Reject Trump’s New Political Map
articles.abstract: Republicans hold an overwhelming majority in the Indiana Senate, but more than a dozen of them defied the president’s wishes, voting against a map aimed at adding Republicans in Congress.
articles.snippet: Republicans hold an overwhelming majority in the Indiana Senate, but more than a dozen of them defied the president’s wishes, voting against a map aimed at adding Republicans in Congress.

articles.title: The Best-Kept Secret in Washington
articles.abstract: On what makes Congress secret and toxic.
articles.snippet: On what makes Congress secret and toxic.
articles.byline: By Kevin R. Kosar and John Guida

articles.title: Jackson’s Death Arrives at a Crucial Moment for Black Political Power
articles.abstract: There are more Black senators than ever before, but a major Supreme Court ruling could reduce Black representation in the House.
articles.snippet: There are more Black senators than ever before, but a major Supreme Court ruling could reduce Black representation in the House.
articles.byline: By Reid J. Epstein and Nick Corasaniti

articles.title: Trump Sought Vast Budget Cuts. Congress Granted Few.
articles.abstract: In a series of deals over the past three months, lawmakers rejected some of the president’s most aggressive attempts to whittle down the government.
articles.snippet: In a series of deals over the past three months, lawmakers rejected some of the president’s most aggressive attempts to whittle down the government.

articles.title: How the Decision to Start a War Became the President’s
articles.abstract: Presidents have sidestepped Congress to launch limited military strikes for decades. Trump’s decision to attack Iran is an aggressive escalation.
articles.snippet: Presidents have sidestepped Congress to launch limited military strikes for decades. Trump’s decision to attack Iran is an aggressive escalation.
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Trump Tramples Congress’s Power, With Little Challenge From G.O.P.
articles.abstract: On national security, spending and oversight, the president continues to undercut the legislative branch, and Republicans in charge have done little to stop him.
articles.snippet: On national security, spending and oversight, the president continues to undercut the legislative branch, and Republicans in charge have done little to stop him.
articles.byline: By Julian E. Barnes and Catie Edmondson
keywords: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

articles.title: Why a G.O.P. War Powers Hawk Was a No on Reining Trump In on Iran
articles.abstract: Many Republicans deferred almost unquestioningly to President Trump after he began a sweeping military offensive in the Middle East. Senator Todd Young was more conflicted, but ended up in the same place.
articles.snippet: Many Republicans deferred almost unquestioningly to President Trump after he began a sweeping military offensive in the Middle East. Senator Todd Young was more conflicted, but ended up in the same place.
